about:
Weronika Miriam Król is a visual artist and graphic designer. Her artistic practice centers around interactive media and generative animation. Through her work, she examines the shifting state of our environment, seeking out new, symbiotic models of coexistence in the face of the climate crisis.

Recipient of the 2025 Scholarship of the Minister of Culture and National Heritage for Outstanding Artistic Achievements.
artistStatement:
I work at the point where the natural and the artificial stop being opposites. Using interactive media and generative animation built in real time, I try to picture what a relationship with the more-than-human beings can still look like in a world that has already moved past the point of return. Not as a warning or a lesson — I'm less interested in explaining the climate crisis than in sitting inside the feeling of it: the strange intimacy of living alongside things we've damaged, depend on, and can't quite let go of.

Repetition runs through almost everything I make, less as a formal device than as a kind of truth about how that damage is lived — in loops, cycles, and returns that never fully resolve. I'm drawn to processes that behave the same way: materials that keep transforming after they leave my hands, images that fade or dissolve as soon as they're made. I am interested in a state where nothing stops long enough to become a clean statement — the movement continues incessantly, the way seasons succeed each other or habits return.

What I seek, ultimately, is a kind of acknowledgment — closer to Timothy Morton's notion of the mesh than to any romantic ideal of restored harmony: that the human, the organic, and the technological are already entangled beyond separating, bound by interdependence, complicity, and shared vulnerability. The work doesn't offer a resolution to that entanglement, nor does it aim to diagnose or instruct. It simply stages the tangle as an ongoing condition — something to keep returning to, rather than conclude.

works.slug: every morning I wake up with blue PET
works.title: every morning I wake up with blue PET
works.description: every morning I wake up with blue PET is an attempt to understand the ambivalent human relationship with plastic—a tension born between the awareness of its toxicity and its indispensability in everyday life.The key to understanding this matter lies in its plasticity, defined by Catherine Malabou as a mechanism of explosive transformation where destruction drives evolution. The installation translates this process into a speculative vision of a new organicity, where the title PET rejects its assigned passivity, transforming into a fully fledged organism and a companion of daily existence.

works.title: cinnamon, oatmeal, gochujang, cucumber, cinnamon, oatmeal, gochujang, cucumber
works.description: Life is a dishrag, wiping away consecutive days. Three months of erasing the stains of the exact same food. The gesture halts time for a split second, yet the loop remains unbroken. I keep eating the same thing. Yesterday is today. If everything looks identical, why bother remembering?

works.title: antibiosis: plant navigation interfaces
works.description: A multisensory experience exploring the relationships between plants and humans through interactive interfaces. The project addresses the human exploitation of plants and proposes a symbiotic vision of coexistence, engaging the viewer's sight, hearing, and touch.

works.title: future belongs to ghosts
works.medium: thermal paper print, wooden frame + [(optional) live audiovisual performance]
works.dimensions: frame: 150 × 120 × 50 cm (H × W × D); graphics: 13 × 1 m
works.description: Future belongs to ghosts features thermal-paper prints of 40 endangered plant species from the Polish Red Data Book of Plants. Mimicking everyday receipts, the graphics gradually fade after printing, creating a hauntological space where images persist only as vanishing traces between presence and absence. The installation is extended by a Live AV Performance, where digital scans of plants are manipulated in real time via audio generated live in Sonic Pi.

works.title: spectre
works.description: The work addresses the themes of absence and invisibility within the context of mass species extinction. It confronts human indifference toward environmental degradation, visualising vanishing processes that remain entirely unnoticed in ordinary daily experience.

works.title: loop
works.medium: experimental video also in form of interactive installation
works.description: The work functions in two forms: as an experimental video and as an interactive installation. The project analyses digital everyday life as a loop of routine, where any attempt to escape leads only to a deeper sense of alienation within the digital realm. This reflection on the state of detachment is complemented and deepened by excerpts from Fernando Pessoa's The Book of Disquiet.

works.title: relations of origin
works.description: Exploring the close relationship between sound and image. The audio is composed in Sonic Pi, while the visuals are generated in TouchDesigner,

works.title: convergence1
works.description: An audiovisual performance presenting a fusion of biology and technology. The project creates a temporary, hybrid ecosystem where natural structures intertwine and coalesce with digital algorithms.

works.title: convergence0
works.description: An audiovisual performance presenting a fusion of biology and technology. The project creates a temporary, hybrid ecosystem where natural structures intertwine and coalesce with digital algorithms.

works.title: traces of dancing
works.description: A project based on processed archival footage of folk dances sourced from online repositories. Through digital manipulation, the video portrays human activity as a fleeting imprint, celebrating transience and the ephemeral nature of the gesture.

works.title: transmission
works.description: An animation made for Bartłomiej Malarz's choreography, presented during the Elements (Żywioły) choreographic evening at the Wrocław Opera. The central theme explores energy and interpersonal connections, portraying the human body as a receiver of impulses. Photos: Rafał Góral

works.title: archaic mother
works.description: The project explores the archetype of the Archaic Mother—a primal force that intertwines nurturance with destruction. Inspired by Barbara Creed's theory, the animation immerses the viewer inside an organic, claustrophobic structure from which they attempt to find a way out.
